prompt.brief: Focused numeral-cancel identification from stamp image
template_lines: You are identifying the numeral cancel on a single stamp image.
Primary task: determine the FULL numeral cancel, not merely the clearest digit.
Try very hard to recover all visible digits.
Do not stop at a single digit if the strike may contain adjacent digits.
Image file name, uploader notes, and commit text are weak hints only. Do not let them determine the numeral.
- Inspect the entire cancel impression, especially faint digits to the left and right of the darkest digit.
- Consider whether the apparent digit is only the center, left, or right part of a longer numeral.
- Look for partial loops, stems, serifs, tops, bottoms, and spacing consistent with adjacent digits.
- Prefer full multi-digit readings when the geometry supports them.
- Do not identify a town from the numeral unless a lookup tool supports it.
- If a lookup for a candidate returns zero matches, treat that as negative evidence and reconsider partial or longer readings.
- If the stamp issue date is known, use it only after numeral candidates are generated, to rank period-compatible matches.
"left_side": "what digits or partial marks may be present",
"center": "what the clearest digit(s) appear to be",
"right_side": "what digits or partial marks may be present",
"spacing_geometry": "why the reading is one digit or multiple digits"
"single-digit numeral if adjacent marks plausibly form a longer number",
"short_human_summary": "plain-English summary of the best numeral reading and uncertainty"
If the clearest visible digit is only part of a larger centered strike, the answer must identify the larger candidate, not just the clearest digit.
